Pre-industrial societies worked this way—you built your house, planted your orchard. Money abstraction created the consistency requirement; removing it restores compatibility with volatile lives.",

solution.label: Expenditure Bonding
solution.design_principles: Retire from needing money, not from work. Convert volatile income directly into locked future goods/services during flush periods. Stability through molecular bonding to real resources, not abstract accumulation.
solution.core_mechanism: Workers purchase 'expenditure futures'—legally binding rights to specific future goods (housing, healthcare, food) at today's prices—instead of saving money. Volatility becomes opportunity: high-income months bond to real assets.
solution.how_it_works: Create standardized 'expenditure future' instruments: (1) Housing futures—buy guaranteed occupancy rights in housing cooperatives, purchasable in month-increments (2) Care futures—buy into healthcare mutuals that guarantee lifetime care for fixed buy-in (3) Sustenance futures—invest in agricultural land trusts providing perpetual food rights (4) Utility bonds—pre-purchase lifetime electricity/water. Gig workers buy these opportunistically during high-income periods. A platform tracks coverage gaps and prioritizes purchases. No need for consistent contributions—just convert income to locked goods when available. Research needed: legal frameworks for multi-decade service obligations, cooperative governance models, default insurance mechanisms.
solution.what_is_new: Existing systems assume you need money in old age—this assumes you need goods/services. By directly securing future consumption rather than accumulating abstract medium, you eliminate money→goods conversion volatility and inflation risk entirely.
solution.why_it_works: Volatile earners can't maintain consistent savings, but they CAN make opportunistic purchases. This converts the bug (income spikes) into a feature (buying opportunities). Pre-industrial societies worked this way—you built your house, planted your orchard. Money abstraction created the consistency requirement; removing it restores compatibility with volatile lives.
solution.why_it_fails: Massive coordination problem: requires building parallel economic infrastructure (housing coops, healthcare mutuals, food trusts) at scale before individuals can participate. Early adopters face illiquidity risk—you can't unbuy futures if circumstances change. Service providers may default on 30-year obligations. Geographic lock-in conflicts with mobile workforce.
solution.medium_term: Housing, healthcare, and food cooperatives emerge with standardized 'future' instruments. Gig platforms integrate 'expenditure purchase' options alongside payment. New regulatory category for multi-decade service obligations. Early adopters demonstrate model viability.
solution.long_term_vision: Retirement becomes orthogonal to income consistency. Security is measured in 'years of needs covered' not dollars saved. Cooperative infrastructure provides baseline existence independent of employment. The question 'can you afford to retire?' becomes 'have you bonded enough futures?'—answerable regardless of income volatility. Money becomes optional for basic security.
